content.problemStatement:
Given a list of NNN batsmen and MMM bowlers, each with a given cricketing skill, the selectors are asked to pick a team of 111111 players such that the team has **at least** 444 batsmen and 444 bowlers.

Find is the **maximum** total cricketing skill of a team which they can form.

Note that the total cricketing skill is the sum of cricketing skills of all the players in the team.

Print −1-1−1 if making a valid team is not possible.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM — the number of batsmen and bowlers respectively.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers, denoting the cricketing skills of the NNN batsmen.
The third line contains MMM space-separated integers, denoting the cricketing skills of the MMM bowlers.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **maximum** total cricketing skill of any valid team, or −1-1−1 if no such team exists.
constraints: The sum of NNN and the sum of MMM over all test cases won't exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Selectors can pick all the players apart from the batsmen with skill 111.
Test case 222: Selectors have no choice but to pick all the players.
Test case 333: Selectors have no way to form a team with at least 444 bowlers.
